core_concept: The Human Body: Parts, Needs, and Growth
facts: The heart pumps blood to all parts of the body; you can feel your heart beating faster when you run because your muscles need more blood.
Your lungs take in air when you breathe; the air gives your body the oxygen it needs to stay alive.
Humans grow from babies to children to adults over many years; babies cannot walk or talk, but with food and water they grow bigger and learn new things.
Your tongue is a sense organ that helps you taste; your eyes see, ears hear, nose smells, and skin feels touch.
misconceptions: Some children think the heart is for feeling love or emotions, but the heart's real job is to pump blood through the body.
Some children think babies grow into adults very quickly, like in a few days, but growing from a baby to an adult takes many years of eating, drinking, and sleeping.
